div1.entryFree: Bow-string, string of a bow: he hath twice or thrice cut Cupid's b. Ado III, 2, 11. hold or cut --s, Mids. I, 2, 114, == come what come may. Capell: 'When a party was made at butts, assurance of meeting was given in the words of that phrase; the sense of the person using them being, that he would hold or keep promise, or they might cut his bowstrings.' (?)*
